sn22.85:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was dwelling at Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
sn22.85:1.2: Now at that time a bad view of this kind had arisen in a _bhikkhu_ named Yamaka:
sn22.85:1.3: “As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:10.2: do you regard the Tathāgata as _rūpa_ … _vedanā_ … _saññā_ … _saṅkhārās_ … consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*]?”
sn22.85:11.2: do you regard the Tathāgata as this: without _rūpa_ … without _vedanā_ … without _saññā_ … without _saṅkhāra_ … without consciousness [*aviññāṇo*]?”
sn22.85:11.4: “And when, friend Yamaka, the Tathāgata is not being found here and now as a reality and actuality, is it fitting for you to declare:
sn22.85:11.5: ‘As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:12.1: “Formerly, friend Sāriputta, when I did not understand, I had that bad view.
sn22.85:12.2: But now, having heard this teaching of _dhamma_ from Venerable Sāriputta, that bad view has been abandoned, and the _dhamma_ has been directly understood by me.”
sn22.85:13.1: “If, friend Yamaka, they were to ask you thus:
sn22.85:13.15: what is _dukkha_ has ceased, has vanished away.’
sn22.85:13.16: When asked thus, friend, I would answer in that way.”
sn22.85:13.2: ‘That _bhikkhu_ who is an _arahant_, with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*] — after the breakup of the body, after death, what does he become?’
sn22.85:13.3: When asked thus, friend Yamaka, how would you answer?”
sn22.85:13.4: “If they were to ask me thus, friend:
sn22.85:13.5: ‘That _bhikkhu_ who is an _arahant_, with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*] — after the breakup of the body, after death, what does he become?’
sn22.85:13.6: when asked thus, friend, I would answer in this way:
sn22.85:13.9: what is _dukkha_ has ceased, has vanished away.
sn22.85:14.10: What if I were to insinuate myself and deprive him of life?’
sn22.85:14.11: He would approach that householder or householder’s son and say:
sn22.85:14.13: That householder or householder’s son would accept his attendance.
sn22.85:14.14: He would attend on him, rising before him, going to bed after him, ready to do what was wanted, behaving agreeably, speaking pleasantly.
sn22.85:14.15: That householder or householder’s son would trust him even as a friend;
sn22.85:14.16: he would trust him even as an intimate companion;
sn22.85:14.17: and he would place confidence in him.
sn22.85:14.18: But when it occurred to that man:
sn22.85:14.19: ‘This householder or householder’s son has confidence in me,’ then, knowing him to be alone, he would deprive him of life with a sharp weapon.
sn22.85:14.2: Come then, friend Yamaka, I will make a simile for you for a still greater measure of knowledge of this very meaning.
sn22.85:14.3: Suppose, friend Yamaka, there were a householder or a householder’s son who was rich, affluent, and wealthy,
sn22.85:14.5: Then a certain man might arise who wished him harm, wished him no good, wished him no security from bondage, and wanted to deprive him of life.
sn22.85:14.7: ‘This householder or householder’s son is rich, affluent, and wealthy,
sn22.85:14.9: It is not easy to deprive him of life by force.
sn22.85:15.1: What do you think, friend Yamaka?
sn22.85:15.10: Yet the other did not know him, though he was a killer:
sn22.85:15.13: “Just so, friend, an uninstructed ordinary person, who has not seen the noble ones, who is unskilled in the noble _dhamma_, untrained in the noble _dhamma_, who has not seen good persons, who is unskilled in the _dhamma_ of good persons, untrained in the _dhamma_ of good persons,
sn22.85:15.14: regards _rūpa_ as self, or self as possessing _rūpa_; or _rūpa_ as in self, or self as in _rūpa_.
sn22.85:15.18: he regards consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as self, or self as possessing consciousness [*viññāṇa*]; or consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as in self, or self as in consciousness [*viññāṇasmiṁ*].
sn22.85:15.2: When that man approached that householder or householder’s son and said:
sn22.85:15.3: ‘Let me attend on you, _bhante_,’ was he even then not a killer?
sn22.85:15.4: Yet the other did not know him, though he was a killer:
sn22.85:15.6: And when he attended on him, rising before him, going to bed after him, ready to do what was wanted, behaving agreeably, speaking pleasantly, was he even then not a killer?
sn22.85:15.7: Yet the other did not know him, though he was a killer:
sn22.85:15.9: And when, knowing him to be alone, he deprived him of life with a sharp weapon, was he even then not a killer?
sn22.85:16.1: He does not understand as it actually is _rūpa_ as impermanent [*aniccaṁ*]: ‘_rūpa_ is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*].’
sn22.85:16.4: impermanent [*anicce*] _saṅkhārās_ as ‘_saṅkhārās_ are impermanent [*aniccā*]’ he does not understand as it actually is.
sn22.85:16.5: He does not understand as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as impermanent [*aniccaṁ*]: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*].’
sn22.85:17.1: He does not understand as it actually is _rūpa_ as _dukkha_: ‘_rūpa_ is _dukkha_.’
sn22.85:17.5: He does not understand as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as _dukkha_: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is _dukkha_.’
sn22.85:18.1: He does not understand as it actually is _rūpa_ as not-self [*anattaṁ*]: ‘_rūpa_ is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:18.5: He does not understand as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as not-self [*anattaṁ*]: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is not-self [*anattaṁ*].’
sn22.85:19.1: He does not understand as it actually is _rūpa_ as _saṅkhata_: ‘_rūpa_ is _saṅkhata_.’
sn22.85:19.5: He does not understand as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as _saṅkhata_: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is _saṅkhata_.’
sn22.85:2.1: Several _bhikkhus_ heard: “It seems that a bad view of this kind has arisen in a _bhikkhu_ named Yamaka:
sn22.85:2.2: ‘As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.’”
sn22.85:2.3: Then those _bhikkhus_ went to Venerable Yamaka, and on arrival they exchanged greetings with him.
sn22.85:2.4: After exchanging courteous and memorable talk, they sat down to one side. Seated to one side, those _bhikkhus_ said this to Venerable Yamaka:
sn22.85:20.1: He does not understand as it actually is _rūpa_ as a killer: ‘_rūpa_ is a killer.’
sn22.85:20.2: He does not understand as it actually is _vedanā_ as a killer: ‘_vedanā_ is a killer.’ …
sn22.85:20.3: he does not understand as it actually is _saññā_ as a killer: ‘_saññā_ is a killer.’ …
sn22.85:20.4: He does not understand as it actually is _saṅkhārās_ as killers: ‘_saṅkhārās_ are killers.’
sn22.85:20.5: He does not understand as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as a killer: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is a killer.’
sn22.85:21.1: He goes to _rūpa_, clings/feeds [*upādiyati*] to it, and takes a stand on it: ‘It is my self.’
sn22.85:21.5: he goes to consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*], clings/feeds [*upādiyati*] to it, and takes a stand on it: ‘It is my self.’
sn22.85:21.6: These five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*], gone to and clung to, lead for a long time to harm and _dukkha_.
sn22.85:22.1: But a learned noble disciple, friend, who has seen the noble ones … well trained in the _dhamma_ of good persons,
sn22.85:22.2: does not regard _rūpa_ as self, nor self as possessing _rūpa_; nor _rūpa_ as in self, nor self as in _rūpa_.
sn22.85:22.6: he does not regard consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as self, nor self as possessing consciousness [*viññāṇa*];
sn22.85:22.7: nor consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as in self, nor self as in consciousness [*viññāṇasmiṁ*].
sn22.85:23.1: He understands as it actually is _rūpa_ as impermanent [*aniccaṁ*]: ‘_rūpa_ is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*].’
sn22.85:23.5: he understands as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as impermanent [*aniccaṁ*]: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*].’
sn22.85:24.1: He understands as it actually is _rūpa_ as _dukkha_: ‘_rūpa_ is _dukkha_.’
sn22.85:24.5: He understands as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as _dukkha_: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is _dukkha_.’
sn22.85:25.1: He understands as it actually is _rūpa_ as not-self [*anattaṁ*]: ‘_rūpa_ is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:25.5: He understands as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as not-self [*anattaṁ*]: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is not-self [*anattā*].’
sn22.85:26.1: He understands as it actually is _rūpa_ as _saṅkhata_: ‘_rūpa_ is _saṅkhata_.’
sn22.85:26.5: He understands as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as _saṅkhata_: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is _saṅkhata_.’
sn22.85:27.1: He understands as it actually is _rūpa_ as a killer: ‘_rūpa_ is a killer.’
sn22.85:27.4: he understands as it actually is _saṅkhārās_ as killers: ‘_saṅkhārās_ are killers.’
sn22.85:27.5: He understands as it actually is consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] as a killer: ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is a killer.’
sn22.85:28.1: He does not go to _rūpa_, does not cling/feed [*upādiyati*] on it, does not take a stand on it: ‘It is my self.’
sn22.85:28.5: he does not go to consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*], does not cling/feed [*upādiyati*] on it, does not take a stand on it: ‘It is my self.’
sn22.85:28.6: These five aggregates of clinging/feeding [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*], not gone to and not clung to, lead for a long time to benefit and _sukha_.”
sn22.85:28.7: “That is how it is, friend Sāriputta, for those venerables who have such compassionate fellow practitioners, desiring their good, advising them and instructing them.
sn22.85:28.8: And after hearing this teaching of _dhamma_ from Venerable Sāriputta, my mind [*citta*] has been liberated from the _āsavas_ through non-clinging/non-feeding [*anupādāya*].”
sn22.85:3.1: “Is it true, friend Yamaka, that a bad view of this kind has arisen in you:
sn22.85:3.2: ‘As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:3.3: “That is how I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, friends:
sn22.85:3.4: ‘A _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.’”
sn22.85:4.1: “Do not say so, friend Yamaka; do not misrepresent the _Bhagavā_. It is not good to misrepresent the _Bhagavā_. For the _Bhagavā_ would not say:
sn22.85:4.2: ‘A _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.’”
sn22.85:4.3: Yet even while being spoken to by those _bhikkhus_ in this way, Venerable Yamaka still insisted on that bad view, clinging tightly to it and adhering to it, saying:
sn22.85:4.4: “As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:5.1: Since those _bhikkhus_ were unable to detach Venerable Yamaka from that bad view, they rose from their seats and went to Venerable Sāriputta. On arrival, they said this to Venerable Sāriputta:
sn22.85:5.2: “Friend Sāriputta, a bad view of this kind has arisen in a _bhikkhu_ named Yamaka:
sn22.85:5.3: ‘As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.’
sn22.85:5.4: May Venerable Sāriputta go to the _bhikkhu_ Yamaka, out of compassion.”
sn22.85:5.6: Then in the evening, Venerable Sāriputta emerged from retreat and went to Venerable Yamaka. On arrival, he exchanged greetings with Venerable Yamaka … Seated to one side, Venerable Sāriputta said this to Venerable Yamaka:
sn22.85:6.1: “Is it true, friend Yamaka, that a bad view of this kind has arisen in you:
sn22.85:6.2: ‘As I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death’?”
sn22.85:6.3: “That is how I understand the _dhamma_ taught by the _Bhagavā_, friend: as a _bhikkhu_ with _āsavas_ destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], with the breakup of the body, is annihilated, perishes, and does not exist after death.”
sn22.85:7.11: he understands: ‘there is no more of this state of being.’
sn22.85:8.2: do you regard the Tathāgata as _rūpa_?”
sn22.85:8.8: do you regard the Tathāgata as consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*]?”
sn22.85:9.12: do you regard the Tathāgata as in consciousness [*viññāṇasmiṁ*]?”
sn22.85:9.14: “Do you regard the Tathāgata as apart from consciousness [*viññāṇā*]?”
sn22.85:9.2: do you regard the Tathāgata as in _rūpa_?”
sn22.85:9.4: “Do you regard the Tathāgata as apart from _rūpa_?”
